spec_match.rationale: Pragmatic layout mapping navigation wraps—the single most requested CSS orphan layout feature on the web.

interactive.rationale: Outstanding interactivity. Couples a width controller range slider, item count buttons, and a custom JS probe using getBoundingClientRect to dynamically flag and render red orphan warnings.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Perfect alignment. Directly demonstrates how the feature resolves standard multi-row header spacing limitations without custom JS logic.

edge_cases.rationale: Examines dynamic viewport boundaries and layout orphan calculations.

references.rationale: Includes the necessary link directing developers to the central ChromeStatus milestones.

design_system.rationale: Clean outline buttons, monospace tags, and distinct status highlights matching public/styles.css definitions.

summary: The nav-menu is an exceptional, high-fidelity concept demonstration combining dynamic list modifiers, a width slider, and custom layout detection.
